verses.text: 'O God, thou art my God; early will I seek thee,' David says, his soul thirsting in a dry and weary land. His longing for God is like physical thirst. The believer's deepest craving, even in barren places, is for God Himself.

'O God, thou art my God; early will I seek thee,' David says, his soul thirsting in a dry and weary land. His longing for God is like physical thirst. The believer's deepest craving, even in barren places, is for God Himself.
'Thy lovingkindness is better than life,' so he blesses God and is satisfied 'as with marrow and fatness.' God's love outweighs life itself. To know His favour is a feast richer than any earthly joy.
'Thy lovingkindness is better than life,' so he blesses God and is satisfied 'as with marrow and fatness.' God's love outweighs life itself. To know His favour is a feast richer than any earthly joy.
'Thy lovingkindness is better than life,' so he blesses God and is satisfied 'as with marrow and fatness.' God's love outweighs life itself. To know His favour is a feast richer than any earthly joy.

He remembers God on his bed through the night watches, his soul following hard after Him, upheld by God's right hand. His thoughts turn to God in the dark. Wakeful hours become times of clinging close to God.

He remembers God on his bed through the night watches, his soul following hard after Him, upheld by God's right hand. His thoughts turn to God in the dark. Wakeful hours become times of clinging close to God.
Those who seek his life shall sink into the earth, but the king shall rejoice in God, while liars are silenced. Two ends are set. The God who upholds His own will silence every false accuser.
